title: Terraform in CI/CD Pipelines
learningObjectives: Implement a GitOps Terraform workflow with plan on PR and apply on merge
Use saved plan files for reliable CI/CD applies
Configure OIDC authentication for cloud providers in CI/CD
Use Terraform Cloud/Enterprise for remote operations and policy enforcement

theory.content:
CI/CD Workflow for Terraform

The standard Terraform CI/CD workflow:

  1. PR opened → run terraform plan and post results as a comment
  2. PR reviewed and approved → merge to main
  3. Push to main → run terraform apply with saved plan

Saving Plan Files

# In CI: generate plan file
terraform plan -out=tfplan.binary

# Convert to human-readable (for PR comment)
terraform show -json tfplan.binary > plan.json

# In CD: apply the exact saved plan
terraform apply tfplan.binary

GitHub Actions Example

name: Terraform
on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
  push:
    branches: [main]

jobs:
  terraform:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      id-token: write  # For OIDC
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
    
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Configure AWS via OIDC
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          role-to-assume: arn:aws:iam::123456789:role/github-actions-terraform
          aws-region: us-east-1
      
      - name: Terraform Init
        run: terraform init
      
      - name: Terraform Plan
        if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
        run: terraform plan -out=tfplan.binary
      
      - name: Terraform Apply
        if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'
        run: terraform apply tfplan.binary

OIDC Authentication

Instead of long-lived secrets, use OIDC (OpenID Connect) to get short-lived tokens. GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and CircleCI all support OIDC:

  • AWS: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials
  • GCP: google-github-actions/auth
  • Azure: azure/login

Terraform Cloud Integration

# Trigger remote run via API
curl -X POST \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TFC_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/vnd.api+json" \
  -d '{"data":{"type":"runs","relationships":{"workspace":{"data":{"type":"workspaces","id":"ws-xxxxx"}}}}}' \
  https://app.terraform.io/api/v2/runs

Checkov — Security Scanning

# Scan Terraform configuration for security issues
checkov -d . --framework terraform

# Output as SARIF for GitHub Code Scanning
checkov -d . --output sarif --output-file checkov.sarif
